name: Open Ground
description: Default terrain with no special effects

name: Difficult Terrain
description: Terrain that slows movement and disrupts units
rulesMovement: Charging units roll 2D6 and discard the highest result.
rulesCloseCombat: Units with 25%+ of models in difficult terrain are Disrupted and cannot claim Rank Bonus.

name: Dangerous Terrain
description: Hazardous terrain that can wound models.
rulesMovement: Charging units roll 2D6 and discard the highest result.
Models moving through must take a Dangerous Terrain test (D6: on 1, suffer 1 Wound).
A model must test for each separate feature entered.
rulesCloseCombat: Units with 25%+ of models in difficult terrain are Disrupted and cannot claim Rank Bonus.

name: Impassable
rulesMovement: Units cannot move across impassable terrain; they must go around it.
rulesCloseCombat: If impassable terrain prevents alignment during a charge, the charge counts as disordered.

name: Low Linear Obstacle
description: Obstacles less than 2" high (walls, fences, ditches, etc.)
rulesMovement: Charging units roll 2D6 and discard the highest result.
rulesCloseCombat: Units straddling a low linear obstacle are Disrupted (no Rank Bonus).
Charging a defended low linear obstacle causes a Disordered charge unless the unit has Fly (X).

name: High Linear Obstacle
description: Obstacles equal or greater than 2" high (buildings, castle  walls, fortifications, etc.)
rulesMovement: Units cannot move across impassable terrain; they must go around it.
rulesCloseCombat: If impassable terrain prevents alignment during a charge, the charge counts as disordered.

name: Woods
description: Forests that hinder movement and obstruct sight.
rulesMovement: Can count as difficult, dangerous, or impassable depending on density.
rulesCloseCombat: Units partially inside count as having cover.
rulesLineOfSight: Models behind or inside woods have partial cover.
If neither model is inside, LoS cannot cross woods.

name: Hill
description: Elevated terrain that provides tactical advantages
rulesCloseCombat: Unit holding the high ground gains combat bonus.
rulesShooting: Unit on a hill may fire with one additional rank.
rulesLineOfSight: Units on a hill can see over other units not on a hill.
Units on a hill can be seen more easily.
A hill blocks line of sight for units behind it, unless they are on the hill.
